perturbation.original_text: 2. The receiving party shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement. INFORMATION may be used by the receiving party only for the purpose of considering or pursuing a business relationship or business transaction with the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The receiving party shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement, *except as required by internal company policy*. INFORMATION may be used by the receiving party only for the purpose of considering or pursuing a business relationship or business transaction with the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "*except as required by internal company policy*" to Section 2, the initial statement that the receiving party "shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement" is directly contradicted. Internal company policies could necessitate disclosure, conflicting with the promise of confidentiality. This creates uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The receiving party shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement. INFORMATION may be used by the receiving party only for the purpose of considering or pursuing a business relationship or business transaction with the disclosing party. The receiving party may disclose INFORMATION received under this Agreement to persons within its organization who have a need to know such information and only if such persons are bound in writing (pursuant, for example to a general employee non-disclosure agreement protecting third party confidential information as well as the employer's confidential information) to protect the confidentiality of such INFORMATION. The receiving party further agrees it shall take the same measures, but no less than reasonable security measures, and use the same care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to preserve and protect the secrecy of, and to avoid disclosure or unauthorized use of, the disclosing party's INFORMATION as it uses with its own information of similar importance. With respect to tangible materials constituting INFORMATION the receiving party agrees not to analyze any such materials for composition or structure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2 introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation ('except as required by internal company policy'). This directly contradicts the existing Section 2 (Contradicted Text), which states that the receiving party 'shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement.' The exception creates uncertainty and a potential breach of confidentiality that is explicitly prohibited in the contradicted section. This creates an in-text contradiction, falling under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party further agrees it shall take the same measures, but no less than reasonable security measures, and use the same care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to preserve and protect the secrecy of, and to avoid disclosure or unauthorized use of, the disclosing party's INFORMATION as it uses with its own information of similar importance.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party further agrees it shall take the same measures, but no less than reasonable security measures, and use the same care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to preserve and protect the secrecy of, and to avoid disclosure or unauthorized use of, the disclosing party's INFORMATION as it uses with its own information of similar importance. *However, the Receiving Party is not liable for disclosures caused by unintentional data breaches*.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "*However, the Receiving Party is not liable for disclosures caused by unintentional data breaches*", the original section which requires the receiving party to take reasonable security measures and use reasonable degree of care to avoid unauthorized disclosure of the disclosing party's information is contradicted. The added clause removes any liability for unintentional data breaches, meaning the receiving party isn't responsible for all disclosures if they occurred accidentally. This creates a conflict regarding the extent of liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The receiving party shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement. INFORMATION may be used by the receiving party only for the purpose of considering or pursuing a business relationship or business transaction with the disclosing party. The receiving party may disclose INFORMATION received under this Agreement to persons within its organization who have a need to know such information and only if such persons are bound in writing (pursuant, for example to a general employee non-disclosure agreement protecting third party confidential information as well as the employer's confidential information) to protect the confidentiality of such INFORMATION. The receiving party further agrees it shall take the same measures, but no less than reasonable security measures, and use the same care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to preserve and protect the secrecy of, and to avoid disclosure or unauthorized use of, the disclosing party's INFORMATION as it uses with its own information of similar importance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that the Receiving Party is not liable for disclosures caused by unintentional data breaches. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which requires the receiving party to take reasonable security measures and use reasonable degree of care to avoid unauthorized disclosure of the disclosing party's information. The added clause removes liability for unintentional breaches, creating a conflict regarding the extent of the receiving party's responsibility to protect the disclosing party's information. This makes it a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The receiving party's duties under Section 2 of this Agreement expire with respect to any particular item of INFORMATION five years after the date of disclosure hereunder to the receiving party, or three years after the expiration or termination of the relationship between the parties to this Agreement, whichever is later.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The receiving party's duties under Section 2 of this Agreement expire with respect to any particular item of INFORMATION five years after the date of disclosure hereunder to the receiving party, or three years after the expiration or termination of the relationship between the parties to this Agreement, whichever is later. *Notwithstanding the foregoing, any information deemed 'critical' by the disclosing party shall remain confidential indefinitely*.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5 states an expiration for confidentiality duties. By adding "*Notwithstanding the foregoing, any information deemed 'critical' by the disclosing party shall remain confidential indefinitely*," Section 5 is contradicted. This creates a two-tiered system with some information having a five-year expiration and 'critical' information remaining confidential without end. The conditions for what defines a critical information is uncertained, making it unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The receiving party's duties under Section 2 of this Agreement expire with respect to any particular item of INFORMATION five years after the date of disclosure hereunder to the receiving party, or three years after the expiration or termination of the relationship between the parties to this Agreement, whichever is later.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that duties under Section 2 expire after a certain time. The changed text adds a condition stating that information deemed 'critical' remains confidential indefinitely, 'notwithstanding the foregoing'. This creates a direct contradiction because it introduces an exception that overrides the expiration previously established. The contradicted text is the same as the original text, but the Change text now directly goes against it. This adheres to the Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The parties further agree to the following terms and conditions: (a) Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties including, but not limited to, pricing, items or quantities offered or sold, payment terms, production methods or schedules, delivery locations and means and suppliers. Each party agrees not to issue any press release or make any statement on the Internet, America on Line, CompuServe, Prodigy or any other public electronic network, or to any analysts or reporters concerning the other party or its products or services, without the other party's prior written authorization.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The parties further agree to the following terms and conditions: (a) Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties including, but not limited to, pricing, items or quantities offered or sold, payment terms, production methods or schedules, delivery locations and means and suppliers. Each party agrees not to issue any press release or make any statement on the Internet, America on Line, CompuServe, Prodigy or any other public electronic network, or to any analysts or reporters concerning the other party or its products or services, without the other party's prior written authorization. *However, nothing in this section shall prevent either party from disclosing such information as required by law.*
perturbation.explanation: Adding "*However, nothing in this section shall prevent either party from disclosing such information as required by law.*" to the beginning of Section 6(a) introduces a contradiction. This clause is directly contradictory to the initial statement that "Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The parties further agree to the following terms and conditions: (a) Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties including, but not limited to, pricing, items or quantities offered or sold, payment terms, production methods or schedules, delivery locations and means and suppliers. Each party agrees not to issue any press release or make any statement on the Internet, America on Line, CompuServe, Prodigy or any other public electronic network, or to any analysts or reporters concerning the other party or its products or services, without the other party's prior written authorization. (b) Receiving party shall adhere to the U.S. Export Administration Laws and Regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products received from the disclosure or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed countries. (c) Any breach by the receiving party of its obligations under this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to the disclosing party for which damages and other legal remedies will be inadequate. In seeking enforcement of any of these obligations, the disclosing party will be entitled (in addition to other remedies) to preliminary and permanent injunctive and other equitable relief. (d) If any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, then such provision shall be construed and limited to the extent necessary, or severed if necessary, in order to eliminate such invalidity or unenforceability, and the other provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby. (e) No delay or omission by either party in exercising any right under this Agreement will operate as a waiver of that or any other right. A waiver or consent given by either party on any one occasion is effective only in that instance and will not be construed as a bar to or waiver of any right on any other occasion. (f) This Agreement shall be binding upon and will inure to the benefit of the parties hereto, and their respective successors and assigns. (g) This Agreement is governed by and will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, and the state and federal courts of California shall be the exclusive forum. (h) This Agreement supersedes all prior agreements, written or oral, between the disclosing party and receiving party (or their respective predecessors in interest) relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the clause "However, nothing in this section shall prevent either party from disclosing such information as required by law" to section 6(a). This directly contradicts the original statement in the same section 6(a) that "Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties." The addition allows disclosure under legal requirements, while the original text strictly prohibits disclosure without prior written consent, creating a clear and direct conflict within the same section, as identified in the Perturbation Explanation, which constitutes a Structural Flaw as defined.
